name: Kahana Bay
tagline: A quiet crescent of sand tucked into a lush valley — the kind of spot most tourists drive right past.
parking: Small lot off Kamehameha Hwy with additional roadside parking
bestTime: Weekday mornings for near-total solitude; spring and summer for calmer water
talkStory: This is one of the most underrated beaches on the island. The bay is shallow and protected, the mountains wrap around you, and there's hardly anyone here on weekdays. It connects to the Kahana Valley hiking trail if you want to stretch your legs. Locals fish here in the evenings — it's that kind of mellow spot. No frills, just pure windward beauty.
